name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Gender wage gap = national embarrassment
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | Today is one of my least favorite days on the calendar. **It's Equal Pay Day.** Women who work full-time must work all of 2017 and up through today, April 10th, in 2018 to earn what men earned in 2017 alone. On average, women in the United States lose $10,086 per year to the gender wage gap -- or about $840.50 a month. $840.50 a month is enough for groceries. It's a student loan payment. It could cover the mortgage or rent (or at least a sizable chunk of it). For a family living paycheck-to-paycheck, that's a lot of money to be missing from your bank account. To me, the gender wage gap isn't just a glaring economic issue for women and the families they support -- it's a national embarrassment. And we need to do something about it. [_**This Equal Pay Day, help us gather 10,000 signatures before midnight on April 10th to pressure Congress for legislation to close the gender wage gap. Add your name right now >>**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113ff3/622b7b5b/1a283f06/307b8a30/374586249/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180410") Thank you. Debbie

name: Chris McDaniel
subject: The Rebel Rides Again
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Friends of Chris McDaniel
body:
When I challenged Thad Cochran in 2014, the establishment decided I was “stepping out of line.” They thought they could and should control me, and were mad I didn’t do exactly what they wanted. Now in 2018, they have already started dusting off the book of dirty tricks from four years ago to try and stop this conservative movement for once again not doing what they want. Peter, When I challenged Thad Cochran in 2014, the establishment decided I was “stepping out of line.” They thought they could and should control me, and were mad I didn’t do exactly what they wanted. Now in 2018, they have already started dusting off the book of dirty tricks from four years ago to try and stop this conservative movement for once again not doing what they want. Politicians used to love how I would never back down from a fight to defend conservative values. Now they’ve suddenly turned their backs on me because I once again refused to back down. I’m not here to make friends with the establishment. I’m not looking for their favor or to join the good old boys club. I’m here to fight for Mississippi and the conservative values that the establishment has forgotten about. Help me take the fight to the establishment and send a true conservative to Washington. Chip in anything you can -- $10, $25, $50, or even $100 or more -- to show the establishment we won’t let them push us around any longer. [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180410_RebelRides&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180410_RebelRides] Chip in $10 [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180410_RebelRides&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180410_RebelRides] Chip in $25 [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180410_RebelRides&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180410_RebelRides] Chip in $50 [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180410_RebelRides&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180410_RebelRides] Chip in $100 [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180410_RebelRides&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180410_RebelRides] Chip in other amount [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180410_RebelRides&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180410_RebelRides] I’ve always been a fighter and a bulldog for my entire career. The establishment doesn’t like that I won’t “ask for their permission” first to continue fighting for Mississippians. They would rather seek to control us than actually fight for us. I’m here to put an end to their dirty tricks so that the people of Mississippi can finally have the conservative bulldog they deserve representing them in Washington. Stand up to the establishment by chipping in whatever you can afford to my campaign. Together we’ll finally get a real conservative in the Senate fighting for Mississippi. [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180410_RebelRides&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180410_RebelRides] Thank you, Chris P.S. ICYMI: Below is the “Rebel Rides Again” [https://mcdaniel2018.com/2018/04/06/icymi-mississippis-rebel-rides/] article from the US News & World Report that explains why the establishments in Washington and Mississippi go to such lengths to try and stop me. Mississippi's Rebel Rides Again By David Catanese

US News & World Report When state Sen. Chris McDaniel filed a lawsuit in 2010 challenging the constitutionality of Obamacare in Mississippi, Phil Bryant, the lieutenant governor at the time, joined it. When Bryant called for an Arizona-like immigration law to allow police to perform random checks of citizenship, McDaniel took up the cause, drafting the legislation. As governor, Bryant enthusiastically signed a stack of McDaniel-sponsored bills that gave businesses more input on the regulations that govern them, added terrorism to the list of crimes that could trigger the death penalty and protected students' right to express religious preferences at public ceremonies and sporting events. And even when McDaniel broke off with 10 of his Republican colleagues in 2013 to form a conservative Senate coalition designed to evaluate the merits of GOP-led legislation, Bryant was publicly supportive, with his spokesman saying the governor was encouraged by the move and looked forward to "working with them as they fight for fiscal responsibility and conservative values." For years, McDaniel and Bryant enjoyed a mutually beneficial political alliance rooted in conservative political principles in governing one of the reddest states in the country. Now, they're at loggerheads – bitter foes in a face-off over a U.S. Senate seat suffused in recriminations, revenge and redemption that could ultimately determine the balance of power in the upper chamber this fall. Everything began to change when McDaniel announced his campaign to challenge Sen. Thad Cochran in 2013, presenting himself as a fresh face of a new generation who would shun statesmanlike dealmaking and press confrontation in defense of core GOP values. At the outset, Bryant didn't take McDaniel's maverick challenge seriously, essentially waving it off, according to a source close to the governor. He supported Cochran and thought McDaniel's bid was a relatively harmless play to raise his name recognition for a future statewide run. Instead, an influx of outside groups supporting the tea party star turned the primary into a $17 million, nationally tracked, bare-knuckled brawl that became one of the nastiest and most competitive contests the Magnolia State had ever seen. After a harsh campaign featuring a criminal nursing home break-in and a bizarre county courthouse lock-in, McDaniel lost to Cochran in a runoff by just 7,667 votes. Shaken by Cochran's late comeback, he refused to concede, alleging voting irregularities and outright malfeasance at the highest levels of the GOP, prolonging a bitter battle that continues to divide Mississippi Republicans to this day. McDaniel's decision against a graceful concession – "We are not prone to surrender, we Mississippians," he told supporters the night of the runoff – was the "watershed moment when the wheels came off that relationship," a source close to Bryant says. McDaniel, once a rising star, became a radioactive political pariah. "I wasn't the golden child anymore. I was the guy that stepped out of line," McDaniel says now. "I didn't ask for permission so they decided to punish me. Had I not challenged Sen. Cochran, I would still be their golden boy. There's no question about that. The challenge changed the whole trajectory of Mississippi politics from that standpoint." The 45-year-old McDaniel is back, embarking on another gate-crashing campaign that again pits him against Republican establishment forces from the Beltway to Jackson. It's an insurgent bid that was never really in doubt and hadn't ever stopped since his audacious 2014 foray – "He kinda never stopped running," says Rick Tyler, a top McDaniel adviser. But this time, both McDaniel and the Bryant political machine aligned firmly against him are even more dug in, and – given the history – neither side is taking anything for granted. "You learn when you step on a landmine, you know next time where not to step," McDaniel says. "We understand that it's a very vicious cycle, it's a very vicious past that we're up against." Responds the Bryant confidante, "The one thing Phil Bryant loves to do is campaign. He's gonna work his ass off to get [McDaniel opponent] Cindy Hyde-Smith elected. It's going to be hard for Chris to take on." The mischievous maneuvering has already begun. Read the full story here. That's how Florida government works, or at least that's how is supposed to work. There's only one problem, the current CFO was not only hand picked by Governor Scott for the job but he has also been the beneficiary of the Governor's campaign apparatus as he gears up for November. Governor Scott likes to talk about how he is a champion of government transparency. He likes to claim that his government hasn't wasted ANY taxpayer money. Raise your hand if you believe that! The reality is, we don't know if Rick Scott's government hasn't wasted any taxpayer money because the current CFO -- who is supposed to serve as an independent audit in the state Cabinet -- hasn't disclosed how much waste his office has found or even if they've audited any of Florida's $80 BILLION in annual contracts. If there has ever been a time to drain the swamp in Tallahassee, now is that time. **But Rick Scott is only part of that problem.** It's time to put an end to the Republican cronyism that has corrupted the Florida Cabinet. [Drain the swamp in Tallahassee! name: The White House
subject: How President Trump is fixing welfare
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=476de8a29932b6946c066848accf83f99d0f65cb6dc9ecfd3dca7cd4dea56eea13da967928c570c58251bb3feb02859605a7e6cf689a091d) The White House • April 11, 2018
## Driving the Day
| • | **Taking a stand for victims:** This morning, nine Members of Congress join President Donald J. Trump in the Oval Office as he signs the bipartisan Allow Victims to Fight Online Sex Trafficking Act of 2017. [Learn more about human trafficking.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=476de8a29932b6941b3b58cca14ed89dd215d57442229f3a3de1757f2fbd30e8866efb67e36cc3996508e7cadb2062f4315f7727b19a682c)
---|---
• | **Siding with Main Street:** Leaders from the retail industry will stop by the White House today to discuss their role in contributing to the President’s economic growth agenda.
• | **Setting priorities:** President Trump and Vice President Mike Pence will dine with Republican Congressional leaders to discuss shared goals for the months ahead.

---
|

## How President Trump is fixing welfare
**The Executive Order on Economic Mobility,** which President Trump signed late yesterday, takes the first steps toward real welfare reform in America. The last overhaul of the welfare system came more than two decades ago under the Republican Congress’ “Contract with America,” led by Speaker of the House Newt Gingrich.
**Welfare enrollment for able-bodied adults is at a record high** , despite near-record low unemployment in most parts of the country. For example, there are a record 28 million able-bodied adults on Medicaid today—a figure that has quadrupled since 2000.
**Work is the solution**. States that have enacted commonsense work requirements are seeing positive results, improving the lives of thousands. Washington can learn from their example: After reforms in Kansas and Maine, individuals who left welfare and returned to work saw their average incomes more than double.
[President Trump’s reforms will restore independence and dignity to millions. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=476de8a29932b69463c7829f10196e8bcc3cf16919591d9cfc496414eeac455ea0e66bc3867ddc22728207059f3eb3d0845edfb066c74b80)

---
|

## ‘Prescribed to Death’
Beginning today, the National Park Service is partnering with the nonprofit National Safety Council (NSC) to host “Prescribed to Death,” a temporary memorial depicting the 22,000 people who died from prescription opioid overdose in 2015.
**The memorial is being hosted on the Ellipse** just below the White House’s South Lawn. Its purpose is to educate visitors on the devastating impact of America’s opioid crisis, Press Secretary Sarah Sanders said. “These stories are tough to hear, and this exhibit will be an intensely emotional and somber experience," she said. “But it's also a reminder that lives are at stake, and we must take action.”
**The memorial will open to the public tomorrow** and remain on exhibit until next Wednesday, April 18. It arrives less than one month after President Trump announced an aggressive plan to combat the opioid epidemic in America.
[Learn why the NSC is bringing “Prescribed to Death” to the White House.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=476de8a29932b694f376d357339fa8acfcd54b3c11691601e3e8d401ea180f8cf30cfda4ff46e7855de68dfc56762b6644f793614411d127)
**More** : [See President Trump’s plan to stop opioid abuse and cut off the illicit drug supply. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=476de8a29932b694e56e4d0ac01d98f96e950c4f8e527c08a08be01ab3a9ebe6f16859088d3ad586527673f7a18823e823ca47e3d694f036)

---
|

## Photo of the Day
Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead
President Donald J. Trump and the 2017 NCAA Football National Champions the Alabama Crimson Tide

---
|

## What's Next
Later this week, Vice President Pence will travel to South America to represent the United States at the eighth Summit of the Americas. The Vice President traveled to four Latin American countries last August: Argentina, Chile, Colombia, and Panama.

Trump in the Oval Office as he signs the bipartisan Allow Victims to Fight Online Sex Trafficking Act of 2017. ---|--- • | Siding with Main Street: Leaders from the retail industry will stop by the White House today to discuss their role in contributing to the President’s economic growth agenda. • | Setting priorities: President Trump and Vice President Mike Pence will dine with Republican Congressional leaders to discuss shared goals for the months ahead. | How President Trump is fixing welfare The Executive Order on Economic Mobility, which President Trump signed late yesterday, takes the first steps toward real welfare reform in America. The last overhaul of the welfare system came more than two decades ago under the Republican Congress’ “Contract with America,” led by Speaker of the House Newt Gingrich. Welfare enrollment for able-bodied adults is at a record high , despite near-record low unemployment in most parts of the country. For example, there are a record 28 million able-bodied adults on Medicaid today—a figure that has quadrupled since 2000. Work is the solution. States that have enacted commonsense work requirements are seeing positive results, improving the lives of thousands. Washington can learn from their example: After reforms in Kansas and Maine, individuals who left welfare and returned to work saw their average incomes more than double. | ‘Prescribed to Death’ Beginning today, the National Park Service is partnering with the nonprofit National Safety Council (NSC) to host “Prescribed to Death,” a temporary memorial depicting the 22,000 people who died from prescription opioid overdose in 2015. The memorial is being hosted on the Ellipse just below the White House’s South Lawn. Its purpose is to educate visitors on the devastating impact of America’s opioid crisis, Press Secretary Sarah Sanders said. “These stories are tough to hear, and this exhibit will be an intensely emotional and somber experience," she said. “But it's also a reminder that lives are at stake, and we must take action.” The memorial will open to the public tomorrow and remain on exhibit until next Wednesday, April 18. It arrives less than one month after President Trump announced an aggressive plan to combat the opioid epidemic in America. subject: Party in Evansville: 2018 State Convention
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
The countdown is on! It's now just three months until Republicans from across Indiana will gather in Evansville for the 2018 Indiana Republican... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/party-in-evansville-2018-state-convention?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
| | [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b1f1b3bfbb&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| The countdown is on! In less than two months, Republicans from across Indiana will gather in Evansville for the 2018 Indiana Republican State Convention.

This year's state convention will be another historic event, as we hold our convention outside Indianapolis for just the second time in state history. We've got two jam-packed days (June 8-9) of fun planned for you, starting with our #INDGOP18 Friday Night Party at the Ford Center in Evansville on the first night of convention.

**The #INDGOP18 Friday Night Party will feature live music, great food and special appearances from our top Indiana Republican elected officials.**

There's only a limited number of delegate spots remaining, so if you didn't run to be a delegate, [fill out this form](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0591027c9f&e=1acd9bb3d6) and let us know that you're interested in joining in on the fun. We'll pass your information along to your local Republican team, and they'll work to get you plugged in on this great event!

We don't want you to miss a great party like this, so once you're confirmed as a delegate in your county, make sure you're there by:
* [Paying your delegate fee](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5f9d9f1b8d&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* [Reserving your hotel room in Evansville for Friday night](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bbcaef74a6&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* [Keeping up-to-date with the latest convention details](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d46050521f&e=1acd9bb3d6)

With the excitement we're seeing from hundreds of Hoosier Republicans about convention, we've already sold out multiple hotels in downtown Evansville -- totaling more than 370 rooms! **Special discounted rooms are still available at the Courtyard by Marriott Evansville East and the Hilton Garden Inn Evansville. However, these rooms are also expected to sell out, so ****make sure to reserve your room _now_.**

And once you're in Evansville, you'll want to check out the wide range of restaurants and things to do near the Ford Center, and we've put together [a great guide to get you started](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f29e798a5f&e=1acd9bb3d6).

If you have any additional questions, please feel free to reach out. We look forward to seeing you as we get the party started in Evansville on Friday, June 8, at the 2018 Indiana Republican State Convention!
---
|
---

name: Tom Wolf
subject: re: Dwight Evans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom Wolf for GovernorContributions or gifts to Tom Wolf for Governor are not tax deductible
body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- [](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecc3e/73a2bc12/3cc0fd2d/1fcd0403/265508235/VEsH/ "http://wolfforpa.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=tomwolf&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180410_wolf_fnd_gq_act__tw&source=em18_180410_wolf_fnd_gq_act__tw") Hello --

I've known Dwight Evans for a long time -- and he's always worked block-by-block to revitalize neighborhoods and be a voice for working people.

In Congress, Dwight has led the fight against President Donald Trump and Speaker Paul Ryan's extreme agenda that rewards special interests at the expense of everyday Americans. He's fought to protect Obamacare, which has helped more than 200,000 Philadelphians gain access to affordable health care through Medicaid expansion and the insurance exchanges.

Dwight's brought grocery stores to underserved neighborhoods, and he's fought back against attempts by Paul Ryan to cut SNAP benefits that nearly 500,000 Philadelphians rely on to feed themselves and their families.

[**With Donald Trump in the White House, we need strong allies like Dwight in Congress to make sure this administration doesn't get away with attacks on working Pennsylvanians. That's why it's critical that you stand with Dwight and help him win re-election this November with a donation of $3 or more.**](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecc3e/73a2bc12/3cc0fd2d/1fcd0402/265508235/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/evans-wolf-2018?utm_campaign=em18_180410_wolf_fnd_gq_act__tw&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&refcode=em18_180410_wolf_fnd_gq_act__tw&utm_content=2+-+With+Donald+Trump+in+the+White+House+we+")

Dwight is the congressman we need to make Philadelphia stronger, block-by-block -- by investing in education, passing paid sick leave, taking on the NRA and gun lobbyists to tackle gun violence, protecting women's rights, and making sure that immigrants feel welcomed here.

Folks like you came together in 2016 to send Dwight Evans to be a champion for Philadelphia's working people, and that's exactly what he's done. Now it's on us to make sure he can keep fighting for good jobs, high-quality public schools, and strong neighborhoods.

[**Chip in $3 or more to Dwight's campaign and help him fight for you and your family as he takes on Donald Trump, Paul Ryan, and their allies.**](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecc3e/73a2bc12/3cc0fd2d/1fcd0401/265508235/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/evans-wolf-2018?utm_campaign=em18_180410_wolf_fnd_gq_act__tw&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&refcode=em18_180410_wolf_fnd_gq_act__tw&utm_content=3+-+Chip+in+3+or+more+to+Dwights+campaign+an")

Thank you,

Tom Wolf

name: Bob Fertik, Democrats.com
subject: Every American deserves healthcare
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for Texas
body:
| | |
---|---|---
| Dear Activist, Eight years ago, we did something unprecedented.

Congress passed the Affordable Care Act and provided health care coverage to nearly 20 million men, women, and children. And now that we've seen its impact on our lives, the program is more popular than ever.

Yet every year since the ACA became the law of the land, the GOP has made it their mission to repeal it. President Trump declared that it would be his first priority when he took office, and he’s worked in lock step with Republicans like Senator Ted Cruz to launch attack after attack on the program.

[**I’m running for the U.S. Senate because I believe that health care should be a right, not a function of luck or circumstance. And I believe the ACA should be defended, not torn apart. Add your name right now and join me in protecting health care for our families.**](http://mail.democrats.com:81/CT00162601MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2018-04-11)

When Trump tried to rip away coverage from millions, people spoke out and stopped them. But we know they’re working on new ways to undermine the insurance marketplaces, discourage enrollment and chip away at the program over time.

That’s why we all must continue speaking out in support of the ACA and its life-changing impact on our communities. And we need to expand on our gains in coverage by strengthening Medicare and Medicaid and ultimately achieve universal health care coverage.

My campaign is about people, not special interests. I know we are more powerful when we stand together, and -- like passing health care reform eight years ago, or running for office today -- we cannot make progress on our own.

[**If you agree that every Texan, and every American, deserves access to quality, affordable health care, add your name today.**](http://mail.democrats.com:81/CT00162602MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2018-04-11)

Thank you,

Beto O’Rourke [**SIGN UP**](http://mail.democrats.com:81/CT00162603MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2018-04-11)

That’s why we all must continue speaking out in support of the ACA and its life-changing impact on our communities. And we need to expand on our gains in coverage by strengthening Medicare and Medicaid and ultimately achieve universal health care coverage. My campaign is about people, not special interests. I know we are more powerful when we stand together, and -- like passing health care reform eight years ago, or running for office today -- we cannot make progress on our own. name: Jay Inslee
subject: Fire Pruitt [NOW]
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
It's time. Tell Trump to fire Scott Pruitt. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79f4a/2f5ea88d/725ff6e0/2cbcb552/3518065774/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/fire-pruitt?utm_source=ema_2018-04-11_fire-pruitt-resend&source=ema_2018-04-11_fire-pruitt-resend&utm_campaign=fire-pruitt-resend&utm_medium=ema") Friend, Can you imagine a worse person to be in charge of the Environmental Protection Agency than Scott Pruitt? I sure can't. Every day brings fresh details of Pruitt's abuse of his position -- and our trust. Pruitt is putting the EPA up for sale to the highest corporate or special interest bidder. He's rolled out the welcome mat at the EPA for fossil fuel lobbyists and climate change deniers. But Scott Pruitt's been wrong for the job from the very beginning, because of his denial of basic science and his determination to do nothing in the face of it. [**It's time to end the selling out of the health of our kids and communities to the richest polluters. Join me in demanding President Trump fire Scott Pruitt. Add your name here.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79f4a/2f5ea88d/725ff6e0/2cbcb552/3518065774/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/fire-pruitt?utm_source=ema_2018-04-11_fire-pruitt-resend&source=ema_2018-04-11_fire-pruitt-resend&utm_campaign=fire-pruitt-resend&utm_medium=ema") Pruitt is thumbing his nose at Congress, refusing to be held accountable for his environmentally devastating actions. You and I know that climate change is one of the great moral challenges of our time, and we need honest and accountable leadership at the EPA now more than ever. Instead, Pruitt believes he can carry on with impunity, rolling back environmental safeguards that keep our neighborhoods healthy, our air clean, and our water safe to drink, while frequently meeting with industry representatives behind closed doors. With so much at stake for our air, water, natural resources, health and safety, Scott Pruitt must be held fully accountable for his destructive misdeeds. [**Sign on now. Demand President Trump fire Scott Pruitt and take politics out of the EPA and its work.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79f4a/2f5ea88d/725ff6e0/2cbcb552/3518065774/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/fire-pruitt?utm_source=ema_2018-04-11_fire-pruitt-resend&source=ema_2018-04-11_fire-pruitt-resend&utm_campaign=fire-pruitt-resend&utm_medium=ema") Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79f4a/2f5ea88d/725ff6e0/2cbcb552/3518065774/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/fire-pruitt?utm_source=ema_2018-04-11_fire-pruitt-resend&source=ema_2018-04-11_fire-pruitt-resend&utm_campaign=fire-pruitt-resend&utm_medium=ema")

| | Hello Supporters and Friends, Donna has been all over the county to meet business owners, community leaders, and residents. Here are some highlights from last week: Donna rolled up her sleeves and helped out with Alsop Farms' Feeding 5,000 Community Garden project at Gateway Senior Living Center. Donna spoke at the Reid Temple AME Church Prison Ministry: Expunging Criminal Records and Services and Training with nonprofit organizations and citizens looking for expungement. Donna shared her vision for a better Prince George's with the Bradbury Heights Civic Association in Capitol Heights, Maryland.
Donna met with African Union Mission Ambassador Dr. Arikana Chihombori-Quao to discuss strengthening business opportunities in Prince George's County and abroad.

Donna also visited The Sanctuary at Kingdom Square, where Anthony G. Maclin is Senior Pastor, for their Sunday worship service.
Donna joined her sorors at the Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. Tau Delta Zeta Chapter UniTEA at Renditions Golf Course in Davidsonville, MD. Want to have Donna attend your next community meeting? Please email us at [scheduling@donnaforprincegeorges.org](mailto:scheduling@donnaforprincegeorges.org).

Hi there,
Yesterday, Mark Zuckerberg sat before the United States Congress and attempted to explain how 87 million users' data got in the hands of bad actors, including Russia, and undermined our expectations for privacy and the sanctity of our elections.
Senator Hassan held Zuckerberg's feet to the fire in yesterday's hearings, but she needs to know that you're with her. [**Add your name to join Senator Hassan in demanding concrete steps from Facebook that address the extremely serious concerns that have been raised about the platform.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf1e/2a380d31/a43d927/f1f903/495547032/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20180410_hold-fb-accountable?source=e20180410_facebook_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Over the past few weeks and years, issues around privacy, technology addiction and mental health, disinformation and fake news, and election integrity have all come to light.
More specifically, we know that the Republican-leaning firm, **Cambridge Analytica, misused the personal data of more than 87 million Americans -- and Facebook failed to prevent it.**
We need to ensure that our social media is secure and transparent, and we need real action and accountability for tech giants like Facebook.
[**Please click here to call on tech CEOs like Zuckerberg to cooperate with Congress to protect our people, our elections, and our privacy.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf1e/2a380d31/a43d927/f1f903/495547032/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20180410_hold-fb-accountable?source=e20180410_facebook_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")

Team Maggie

name: Tina Smith
subject: Stop Trump's cruel deportation agenda
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
We need to protect Minnesota's Liberian community and stop these planned deportations. | [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48447/c1f7015/6973fca4/114300fe/3200978322/VEsH/ "https://www.tinaforminnesota.com/stop-cruel-deportations?utm_source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&utm_campaign=liberian-deportations&utm_medium=ema") Dear Friend, When we talk about deportations, we need to acknowledge the huge networks of people they affect. During a brutal civil war, thousands of Liberians fled to the U.S. They found asylum, many right here in Minnesota. Years later, now that they have set up lives and families, Trump wants to send them back. Trump's decision to end deportation protections for Liberians will rip families apart. It goes against who we are as a nation. It means we'll lose our friends, community leaders, employees, and innovators who make Minnesota -- and our country -- a better place. [**Sign on now: We must prevent these unfair and unnecessary deportation plans.**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48447/c1f7015/6973fca4/114300fe/3200978322/VEsE/ "https://www.tinaforminnesota.com/stop-cruel-deportations?utm_source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&utm_campaign=liberian-deportations&utm_medium=ema") [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48447/c1f7015/6973fca4/114300fe/3200978322/VEsF/ "https://www.tinaforminnesota.com/stop-cruel-deportations?utm_source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&utm_campaign=liberian-deportations&utm_medium=ema") For those individuals whose deportation protections are ending, their lives are about to become much harder -- they'll be sent back to a country still struggling to recover from war, with few economic opportunities. But it's not just they who will be affected. It's their children, who may have been born into this country as U.S. citizens and may now be separated from their parents. It's their aging parents, who may have no one else to care for them. It's their employees, who will lose jobs when their small businesses close. The Liberian community in Minnesota is over 30,000 strong. No matter who is threatened by unfair deportation, the whole community will be affected. [**It's time for us to step up and protect our neighbors from these cruel deportation plans. Sign on now to stop Trump's deportation agenda and keep our Liberian community safe.**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48447/c1f7015/6973fca4/114300fe/3200978322/VEsC/ "https://www.tinaforminnesota.com/stop-cruel-deportations?utm_source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&utm_campaign=liberian-deportations&utm_medium=ema") Minnesota is as much their home as yours or mine. In our nation of immigrants, we must stand together. Thank you, Tina Smith | [SIGN ON »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48447/c1f7015/6973fca4/114300fe/3200978322/VEsD/ "https://www.tinaforminnesota.com/stop-cruel-deportations?utm_source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&source=ema_2018-04-11_liberian-deportations&utm_campaign=liberian-deportations&utm_medium=ema")

Hi Peter, I'm Mike -- Joe's political director. You might recognize me as the guy who occasionally drives the RV when Joe can't be behind the wheel himself, but my primary role is to know the ins and outs of Indiana -- our local leaders, the issues that uniquely matter to Hoosier voters, and what makes our communities tick.
**Well, I'm reporting back with the view from the ground, and I have to level with you -- this is a tough race.** Hoosiers know that Joe's fought for them in Washington over the last five years, but with over $6 million in Koch-sponsored ads blasting the airwaves, opinions can change quickly.
**And what's more, Republicans haven't even picked their candidate yet. Once they do, we WILL be faced with a FLOOD of negative ads that could _put this entire campaign in jeopardy._ **

**We have to be ready. We need you with us.**

**Will you give whatever you can -- whether it's $3 or $300 -- to help us prepare for the wave of attacks that's coming? **

These not-so-little things are what's going to win us this election and send Joe back to the Senate to fight for Indiana. **But we can 't afford them unless our supporters step up.**

Mike

Yesterday, we held our statewide grassroots organizing kickoff in Orlando with Representative Carlos Guillermo Smith.

Let's just say that we had a lot of fun -- including the hundreds of you who tuned in at one of the 30+ house parties around the state or on your device!

From talking about his parents' incredible hard work to give him a better shot at life -- to his bold, $1 billion plan to invest in our students, teachers, and schools -- to his victories over the gun lobby, Andrew told us loud and clear why everyday Floridians need a champion like him in the Governor's Mansion.

Peter,

Are you ready to flip another Republican-held House seat?

Coming off of Democrats' win with Conor Lamb in Pennsylvania's 18th, we've endorsed another incredibly exciting House candidate in a special election: Hiral Tipirneni in Arizona's 8th.

Before April is over, she could replace former Republican Rep. Trent Franks, an anti-choice extremist who resigned from Congress amid allegations that he asked women on his staff to be surrogate mothers. Yes, you read that right.

This special election is _less than two weeks away._

So if we want Hiral to win, we have to build momentum around her campaign now.

**[Donate $3 to Hiral Tipirneni today, and 100% of your contribution will go straight to her campaign.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5dc3/24c69fd9/1deab62fd/188b4763/1049920011/VEsE/ "https://emilyslist.org/donate/candidate/hiral-tipirneni?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+Hiral+Tipirneni+today+and+10&utm_campaign=WQEFCP28N_digital_email_20180411_HiralEndo_inact&source=WQEFCP28N_digital_email_20180411_HiralEndo_inact")**

Hiral is a remarkable candidate who has what it takes to flip this seat.

She's a proud immigrant -- her parents came to the U.S. from India when she was 3 years old. A bout of typhoid fever as a child inspired her to become a doctor specializing in emergency medicine, and she's a powerful advocate for affordable health care coverage for all.

Hiral's running in a district that Democrats haven't contested since 2012. And winning this race would significantly boost our chances of flipping the House in November.

Hiral's strong campaign is clearly making Republicans nervous. House Majority Leader Kevin McCarthy has already hosted a fundraiser for her opponent, and Paul Ryan is headlining another.

With the GOP giving everything they've got to hold this seat, we need to help Hiral fight back.

**Donate $3 to Hiral today -- 100% of your donation goes directly to her campaign:**
